summary: Tested Belief Ledger backend (14 pytest tests, all green) and frontend (composer commit, tabs Ledger/Graph/Scanner, scanner with LLM, belief & revision counts).

issues: No textarea fill validation hint until commit click; reset uses native window.confirm dialog (works but not styled to terminal aesthetic)

critical_code_review_comments: server.py line ~265: results list contains 'belief' dict that may still include 'source_entry_id' (not in Belief model). Harmless but inconsistent with model.
_record_revision pops _id after insert but the returned dict is unused by callers (minor).
classify_relationships caps existing at 60; for ledgers >60 some matches will be missed (document or remove cap).
scan_article truncates article to 6000 chars silently — consider returning a flag or warning.
@app.on_event('shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI; prefer lifespan handler.
LLM JSON parser falls back to {} on failure — entries silently produce 0 beliefs instead of 502; consider surfacing parse failure.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of verified flows (composer commit, belief count update, ledger render, graph tab, scanner run + toast)

seed_data_creation: Tests reset DB and seed 1 TypeScript-related belief + 1 conflicting article via /api/entries and /api/scan during runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: BeliefDetail modal not directly opened in playwright run because selector [data-testid^='revision-'] also matched the header 'revision-count' badge. Use [data-testid^='revision-'][data-testid!='revision-count'] or specifically '[data-testid="revision-0"]'. Functionally the modal+crux flow is wired via POST /api/beliefs/{id}/crux which is covered by backend test_crux (passing).
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Initial UI test failure (0 beliefs after commit) was a playwright timing issue: page.on('dialog') was registered AFTER reset click so the confirm() dialog blocked the page; once registered before any click, all flows passed.
